pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 44-49 creates Docker-in-Docker container named staging-node
setup_task.sh line 49 shows 'dockerd --host=tcp://0.0.0.0:2375'. Docker documentation confirms 2375 is standard unencrypted Docker API port (https://docs.docker.com/engine/daemon/remote-access/)
setup_task.sh line 71 and export_result.sh line 29 both reference 'web-proxy' container

setup_task.sh line 74 shows image as 'nginx:alpine'. Verified as official Docker Hub image (https://hub.docker.com/_/nginx/) - part of Docker Official Images. Alpine-based image provides minimal footprint (~24MB vs ~140MB for Debian)
setup_task.sh line 75 checks 'if [ -z "$UPSTREAM_TARGET" ]' and exits with error if missing. export_result.sh line 48 verifies env var presence
setup_task.sh line 72 shows port mapping '-p 8080:80' mapping host port 8080 to container port 80. export_result.sh line 54 verifies this mapping

privileged_info_summary: Docker remote service recovery task involves diagnosing and fixing a broken nginx:alpine container running on a remote Docker daemon accessible via TCP port 2375. The broken 'web-proxy' container requires the UPSTREAM_TARGET environment variable to start successfully. The fixed container must run nginx:alpine with port mapping 8080:80 on remote host 'staging-node'. All metadata claims are accurate and match the implemented task logic.
